pr.title: Setup DTK matches in lb files
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Configured DTK split boundaries for a large run of melee/lb source files, cleaned up duplicate or incorrect object declarations in configure.py, and renamed several placeholder symbols so lb files and quatlib references could be matched more cleanly. The most visible symbol improvement was renaming HSD_QuatLib_8037EE0C/fn_8037EE0C to EulerToQuat and updating assembly, C inline asm callsites, symbols.txt, and the quatlib header consistently.
what_changed: Added DTK split entries in config/GALE01/splits.txt for many melee/lb translation units, including lbcommand, lbcollision, lblanguage, lbtime, lb_00B0, lb_00CE, lbvector, lbshadow, lb_00F9, lbarq, lbmemory, lbheap, lbfile, lbarchive, lbdvd, lb_0192, lb_0198, lbsnap, lbgx, lbanim, lbmthp, lbbgflash, lbrefract_data, lbrefract1, lbrefract2, and lbaudio_ax.

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to replace several fn_ or lbl_ placeholder names with file/module-scoped names: lbHeap_80015BD0, lbDvd_8001819C, lb_800195D0, lbBgFlash_80021C48, HSD_QuatLib_8037EB28, HSD_QuatLib_8037EC4C, HSD_QuatLib_8037ECE0, EulerToQuat, HSD_QuatLib_8037EF28, and gmMainLib_804D3EE0.
Renamed the quatlib routine at 0x8037EE0C from HSD_QuatLib_8037EE0C/fn_8037EE0C to EulerToQuat in asm/sysdolphin/baselib/quatlib.s and src/sysdolphin/baselib/quatlib.h.
Updated existing assembly or inline-asm callsites in asm/melee/lb/lb_00F9.s and src/melee/lb/lb_00B0.c to branch to EulerToQuat instead of HSD_QuatLib_8037EE0C.

Removed duplicate/conflicting object entries in configure.py for lbcollision.c, lbarchive.c, and lbrefract3.c, leaving the shown lb files classified as Matching or NonMatching only once in the RuntimeLib object list.
Removed an unused extern declaration for lbl_804D79E0 from src/melee/lb/lbcommand.c.
smart_moves: The PR made split configuration the central enabler before deeper decomp work: adding explicit .text/.data/.rodata/.bss/.sdata/.sbss/.sdata2 ranges gives DTK a reliable map for many lb files at once.

The EulerToQuat rename was applied consistently across symbols.txt, the defining assembly label, the public header declaration, and all visible callers, reducing alias drift between C, asm, and symbol config.

Module-prefixed placeholder names such as lbHeap_80015BD0, lbDvd_8001819C, and lbBgFlash_80021C48 preserved address identity while improving searchability and ownership without pretending to know full semantic names.
Cleaning configure.py duplicates avoids ambiguous build/matching status for the same source file, which is especially important when DTK split boundaries are being introduced.
Removing the unused lbcommand extern avoided carrying a stray local label declaration into a matching setup pass.
decomp_lessons: When introducing DTK matches for a subsystem, add complete split ranges for every relevant section, not only .text, so data and small-data placement can be compared accurately.
A known semantic name like EulerToQuat should replace address-derived placeholder names everywhere in one PR: symbols file, assembly labels, headers, and callsites must stay synchronized.
If full semantics are not known, use module/address names rather than generic fn_ names; this improves ownership tracking while remaining evidence-grounded.
Before marking files for matching, audit configure.py for duplicate Object entries or contradictory Matching/NonMatching declarations for the same path.
Small cleanup such as deleting unused externs can matter during matching because extra declarations may hide whether a symbol is truly required by the source.
naming_conventions: Quaternion library unknowns use HSD_QuatLib_<address> when only module ownership is known, as seen for HSD_QuatLib_8037EB28, HSD_QuatLib_8037EC4C, HSD_QuatLib_8037ECE0, and HSD_QuatLib_8037EF28.
Use a semantic routine name when evidence supports it: HSD_QuatLib_8037EE0C was renamed to EulerToQuat.
For partially identified lb functions, use subsystem/module prefixes plus address, e.g. lbHeap_80015BD0, lbDvd_8001819C, lbBgFlash_80021C48, and lb_800195D0.
Data symbols can also receive module ownership prefixes while retaining address identity, e.g. gmMainLib_804D3EE0 replacing lbl_804D3EE0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: DTK split setup added per-file start/end ranges matching the linked address layout for lb files, enabling object-level matching over a broad library region.
Assembly branch labels were renamed directly without changing instruction bytes; bl HSD_QuatLib_8037EE0C became bl EulerToQuat at the same target address.
Inline asm in C files was updated to the new symbol name while preserving the recorded instruction/comment addresses, maintaining correspondence to the original binary.
configure.py was simplified to avoid repeated object entries for the same file, which prevents build configuration ambiguity during matching.
searchable_terms: Setup DTK matches in lb files
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that decompile or match the lb files whose split ranges were added here.
Check whether EulerToQuat receives a fully decompiled C implementation later or remains assembly-backed after this rename.
Search for follow-up cleanup around lbrefract3.c, since this PR removed both NonMatching and Matching configure.py entries shown in the diff without adding a split entry for that file.
Audit subsequent symbols.txt changes for the module-prefixed lbHeap/lbDvd/lbBgFlash names to see if they were later semantically renamed.
